heading: Technical requirements and standards; extension of compliance date
label: 1006 Technical requirements and standards; extension of compliance date
notes: References in TextThe effective date of , referred to in subsec. (c)(1), is the date that is 4 years after , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .section 1002 of this titlesection 111(b) of Pub. L. 103–414section 1001 of this titleOct. 25, 1994

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 1 Consultation
children.text: To ensure the efficient and industry-wide implementation of the assistance capability requirements under , the Attorney General, in coordination with other Federal, State, and local law enforcement agencies, shall consult with appropriate associations and standard-setting organizations of the telecommunications industry, with representatives of users of telecommunications equipment, facilities, and services, and with State utility commissions.section 1002 of this title

children.heading: Compliance under accepted standards
children.label: 2 Compliance under accepted standards
children.text: A telecommunications carrier shall be found to be in compliance with the assistance capability requirements under , and a manufacturer of telecommunications transmission or switching equipment or a provider of telecommunications support services shall be found to be in compliance with , if the carrier, manufacturer, or support service provider is in compliance with publicly available technical requirements or standards adopted by an industry association or standard-setting organization, or by the Commission under subsection (b), to meet the requirements of .section 1002 of this titlesection 1005 of this titlesection 1002 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: preclude a telecommunications carrier, manufacturer, or telecommunications support services provider from deploying a technology or service; or

children.label: B
children.text: relieve a carrier, manufacturer, or telecommunications support services provider of the obligations imposed by section 1002 or 1005 of this title, as applicable.
meet the assistance capability requirements of by cost-effective methods;section 1002 of this title
protect the privacy and security of communications not authorized to be intercepted;
minimize the cost of such compliance on residential ratepayers;
serve the policy of the United States to encourage the provision of new technologies and services to the public; and

provide a reasonable time and conditions for compliance with and the transition to any new standard, including defining the obligations of telecommunications carriers under during any transition period.section 1002 of this title

children.heading: Extension of compliance date for equipment, facilities, and services
children.label: c Extension of compliance date for equipment, facilities, and services

children.heading: Petition
children.label: 1 Petition
children.text: A telecommunications carrier proposing to install or deploy, or having installed or deployed, any equipment, facility, or service prior to the effective date of may petition the Commission for 1 or more extensions of the deadline for complying with the assistance capability requirements under .section 1002 of this titlesection 1002 of this title

children.heading: Grounds for extension
children.label: 2 Grounds for extension
children.text: The Commission may, after consultation with the Attorney General, grant an extension under this subsection, if the Commission determines that compliance with the assistance capability requirements under is not reasonably achievable through application of technology available within the compliance period.section 1002 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the date determined by the Commission as necessary for the carrier to comply with the assistance capability requirements under ; orsection 1002 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the date that is 2 years after the date on which the extension is granted.

children.heading: Applicability of extension
children.label: 4 Applicability of extension
children.text: An extension under this subsection shall apply to only that part of the carrier’s business on which the new equipment, facility, or service is used.
